user.title: Set up Signal Lights
user.description:
Create a Signal Lights instance to manage notification colors on your lights.

**How it works:** Register lights as outputs, then define signals with colors and conditions. The highest-priority active signal's color is pushed to your lights. When no signals are active, lights turn off.

update_signal.name: Update Signal
update_signal.description: Update an existing signal's properties. Only provided fields are changed.

init.title: Signal Lights Configuration

data.action: What would you like to do?

add_light.title: Add Light
add_light.description: Choose a light entity to use as a signal output. When a signal is active, this light will show the signal's color at the brightness you set here.

data.brightness: Brightness (how bright this light should be when showing a signal)

remove_light.title: Remove Light
remove_light.description: Select a light to stop using as a signal output. The light will no longer be controlled by Signal Lights.

add_signal.title: Add Signal
add_signal.description:
Define a new signal. Signals are prioritized by their position in the list — signals added first are higher priority. You can reorder them later.

**Trigger types:**
- **Condition** — stays active as long as the condition is true (e.g., door is open)
- **Event** — activates for a set duration then expires (e.g., someone arrives home → show for 60s)

trigger_entity_equals.title: Signal: {signal_name} — Entity equals state
trigger_entity_equals.description:
This signal will activate when the entity's state matches the value you enter.

**Examples:** person.someone = `home`, binary_sensor.door = `on`, input_select.mode = `away`

data.state_value: State value to match (e.g., home, on, open)
data.duration: Duration in seconds (event signals only — ignored for conditions)
data.light_filter: Limit to specific lights (leave empty to show on ALL registered lights)

trigger_entity_on.title: Signal: {signal_name} — Entity is on
trigger_entity_on.description:
This signal will activate whenever the selected entity is **on**. Works with binary sensors, switches, lights, and input booleans.

**Examples:** binary_sensor.back_door (open = on), switch.heater, input_boolean.guest_mode

data.duration: Duration in seconds (event signals only — ignored for conditions)
data.light_filter: Limit to specific lights (leave empty to show on ALL registered lights)

trigger_numeric.title: Signal: {signal_name} — Numeric threshold
trigger_numeric.description:
This signal will activate when a sensor's numeric value crosses a threshold.

**Examples:** Battery below 20%, temperature above 90°F, humidity below 30%

data.direction: Activate when value is above or below the threshold
data.duration: Duration in seconds (event signals only — ignored for conditions)
data.light_filter: Limit to specific lights (leave empty to show on ALL registered lights)

trigger_template.title: Signal: {signal_name} — Advanced template
trigger_template.description:
Write a Jinja2 template that evaluates to `true` or `false`. The signal activates when the template result is truthy.

**Examples:**
`{{ states('sensor.battery') | int < 20 }}`
`{{ is_state('person.someone', 'home') and is_state('input_boolean.alerts', 'on') }}`

data.duration: Duration in seconds (event signals only — ignored for conditions)
data.light_filter: Limit to specific lights (leave empty to show on ALL registered lights)

remove_signal.title: Remove Signal
remove_signal.description: Select a signal to permanently remove.

reorder_signals.title: Reorder Signals
reorder_signals.description: Move a signal to a new position. **Position 1 = highest priority** — it will be shown first when multiple signals are active at the same time.

configure_notifications.title: Configure Notifications
configure_notifications.description:
Optionally send persistent notifications when signals are active. The notification shows the highest-priority active signal name, updates in place (never stacks), and auto-clears when all signals go inactive.

Currently **{current_status}**.

configure_cycling.title: Configure Signal Cycling
configure_cycling.description:
When multiple signals are active for the same light, the light can cycle through all active signals in priority order.

Set **Cycle interval** to the number of seconds each signal is shown before switching. Set to **0** to disable cycling (highest priority signal always wins).

error.invalid_trigger_config: Trigger configuration is invalid. Please check your inputs.

abort.no_lights: No lights configured yet. Add a light first.
abort.no_signals: No signals configured yet. Add a signal first.
abort.not_enough_signals: Need at least 2 signals to reorder.
abort.invalid_trigger_config: Invalid trigger configuration — could not save signal.
